description: Starter knowledge pack for Maesa Tech API keys, webhook delivery, export workflows, and third-party integration reconnection issues.

canonicalProblem.title: API & Integration Issue
canonicalProblem.description: Customers need help with API key ownership, webhook delivery, duplicate webhook subscriptions, export jobs, expired export links, or third-party integration authentication changes.

lessons.title: API key stopped working after team member was removed
lessons.rootCause: The API key was tied to the user account that created it, so removing that team member invalidated the key even though the workspace still exists.
lessons.solution: Explain the account-tied key model, confirm which member created the key, and have an active admin generate a replacement key under a current account.
lessons.customerResponse:
{{greetingLine}}

Thank you for the details. I understand the API key stopped working after a team member was removed from the workspace.

Please confirm whether that user originally created the key. In this setup, keys can be tied to the account that issued them, so removing the owning member can invalidate the credential even while the workspace remains active. Have a current workspace admin create a new key from an active account and update the integration with that new value.

If you are unsure which account owned the credential, reply with the workspace name and the approximate date the old key was created so we can guide the next verification step.

Kind regards,
{{organizationName}} Support Team
signals: token died when member was deleted
lessons.whenToEscalate: Escalate if the key appears to belong to an active account but still fails after ownership and workspace status are verified.
doNotPromise: restoring a deleted user's API key
disclosing credential ownership without verifying the workspace admin

lessons.title: Webhooks not firing silently
lessons.rootCause: Webhook deliveries are being rejected before dispatch because the target authentication or endpoint validation fails, so the customer sees no successful callback at all.
lessons.solution: Confirm the endpoint URL, any auth requirement, and whether a test event reaches the endpoint, then verify delivery expectations from the most recent event type.
lessons.customerResponse:
{{greetingLine}}

Thank you for reporting this. I understand the webhook is not reaching your endpoint and no delivery seems to appear on your side.

Please confirm the exact webhook URL that is configured and whether the endpoint expects a token, signature, or other authentication check before accepting the request. Then trigger a test event if that option is available and compare whether your endpoint logs any incoming attempt at all. If nothing arrives, include the event type you expected and the approximate time you triggered it.

With that information, we can help narrow whether the request is being rejected before successful dispatch.

Kind regards,
{{organizationName}} Support Team
lessons.whenToEscalate: Escalate if a verified endpoint with the correct authentication still shows no incoming attempt from a recent test event.
doNotPromise: that silent delivery means the platform never sent the request
instant replay of events before endpoint verification

lessons.title: Webhook duplicate events
lessons.rootCause: More than one webhook endpoint is subscribed to the same event type, so the customer sees the same event arrive multiple times and mistakes the duplicate subscriptions for a retry bug.
lessons.solution: Review every active endpoint subscription for that event type, then deduplicate the configuration before investigating retries.
lessons.customerResponse:
{{greetingLine}}

Thank you for the explanation. I understand you are receiving duplicate webhook events for the same activity.

Please review all active webhook endpoints in the workspace and confirm whether more than one endpoint is subscribed to the same event type. Duplicate deliveries are often caused by overlapping endpoint subscriptions rather than the same endpoint being retried unexpectedly. If possible, compare the endpoint URLs and event subscriptions side by side and note whether the repeated payloads are being delivered to one endpoint or several.

Once you have that comparison, send us the event type and the endpoints involved so we can guide the next step.

Kind regards,
{{organizationName}} Support Team
signals: receiving the same callback multiple times
lessons.whenToEscalate: Escalate if only one endpoint is subscribed and the same event ID is still being delivered multiple times beyond the expected retry pattern.
doNotPromise: that duplicates are always a platform bug before subscription review
turning off retries globally without checking endpoint setup

lessons.title: Data export timing out on large datasets
lessons.rootCause: The requested export is too large for an immediate browser download path, so the job needs to be treated as a longer-running per-workspace export instead of an inline response.
lessons.solution: Confirm the workspace and export scope, reduce the export if possible, or use the deferred export path that notifies the customer when the file is ready.
lessons.customerResponse:
{{greetingLine}}

Thank you for the context. I understand the export is timing out when you try to download a large dataset.

Please confirm which workspace the export is coming from and what date range or object type you are trying to include. Large exports can exceed the immediate browser-download path, so the best next step is often to reduce the scope first or use the longer-running export flow that prepares the file and notifies you once it is ready. If you already narrowed the range and it still times out, tell us the approximate record volume you are exporting.

That will help us guide the most suitable export path for this workspace.

Kind regards,
{{organizationName}} Support Team
lessons.whenToEscalate: Escalate if a narrowed export or deferred export request still times out for a single workspace after the scope has been confirmed.
doNotPromise: that every export can complete synchronously in the browser
a fixed completion time for very large export jobs

lessons.title: Export download link expired
lessons.rootCause: The export was prepared successfully, but the signed download link has passed its validity window and must be regenerated.
lessons.solution: Have the customer request a fresh export or regenerate the download from the original job if supported.
lessons.customerResponse:
{{greetingLine}}

Thank you for the update. I understand the export file was prepared, but the download link has already expired.

Please return to the export history or the original export notification and request a new download if that option is available. These links are often time-limited for security reasons, so an older link may stop working even though the export job itself completed successfully. If you no longer see the export in history, reply with the approximate time it was created and the workspace it belongs to.

Once we have that information, we can guide the next supported step for generating a fresh link.

Kind regards,
{{organizationName}} Support Team
signals: export email link no longer works
lessons.whenToEscalate: Escalate if a newly generated export link expires immediately or the completed export no longer appears in the job history.
doNotPromise: that an expired link can be reactivated in place
permanent access to old export URLs

lessons.title: Third-party integration disconnected after password or SSO change
lessons.rootCause: The third-party integration lost its stored authentication session when the underlying password or SSO identity changed, so the integration now requires a fresh authorization flow.
lessons.solution: Confirm which identity path changed, reconnect the integration from the active account, and verify that the correct owner performs the re-authentication.
lessons.customerResponse:
{{greetingLine}}

Thank you for explaining the timing. I understand the third-party integration disconnected after a password change or SSO update.

Please confirm which connected app is affected and whether the account owner recently changed their password, switched identity provider settings, or signed in through a different SSO path. In many cases the integration must be re-authorized after those identity changes because the previous token is no longer valid. Have the current integration owner reconnect the app from an active session and complete the authorization flow again.

If the reconnect still fails, reply with the integration name and the exact re-authentication message you see.

Kind regards,
{{organizationName}} Support Team
lessons.whenToEscalate: Escalate if the current owner re-authorizes the integration successfully but the connection still drops again immediately afterward.
doNotPromise: preserving the old integration token after an identity change
reconnecting the app without the current authorized owner

author: Maesa Tech
